title: run,rootless: `enable_ipv6` when using `slirp4netns` for rootless networking
body:
When user is invoking rootless buildah and networking defaults to
slirp4netns always use `enable_ipv6` so users can resolve ipv6 addresses
as well.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3968

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@rhatdan @Luap99 PTAL. This should enable `enable-ipv6` by default. I'll also open a followup to make `slirp` opts configurable from UX but don't know if its urgent.

@Luap99 We can make this configurable from UX and we could also unify `slirp4netns` in `libnetwork` but `unification` looks like a bigger change for this feature, do you think we should do it now or later if more parity features gets requested other than only `enable_v6` and we decide to `unify` in general ? I'm fine with anything whatever maintainers agree with.

I leave this for other to decide but I fixed many bugs twice in buildah and podman because the code is duplicated for no real reason. We have to unify the code for easier maintenance. This will also reduce binary size when more code is shared.

@Luap99 Makes sense I agree with you, i think we can get this in `enable_v6` in just to close this issue but instead open a bigger JIRA spike to `unify` `slirp4netns` networking in `libnetwork` irrespective of this feature WDYT ? But again I'm fine with whatever others agree with.  @rhatdan @nalind PTAL
Lets fix this now in containers/common and then vendor it into buildah and Podman for next release.  No reason to rush it now.
Closing this to unify slirp4nets code into `c/common` so both `podman` and `buildah` could use it.
